commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=35198cf38b963e7213944417732ab3673872c62e)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `tools.toolSearch.mode: "directory"`, prompt-directory generation, missing-tool hydration in agent-core, tests, and docs for deferring most tool schemas while keeping selected tools visible.

PR surface: Source +545, Tests +486, Docs +24. Total +1055 across 18 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the PR finding: source inspection shows directory mode sets `clientToolsCataloged` false for allowlists, while the non-code client-tool path still calls `addClientToolsToToolSearchCatalog`, which returns an empty client tool list when compacted. I did not run a live failing provider turn.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] There is a concrete mechanical repair for the client-tool visibility mismatch, though maintainer API approval is still needed before merge.

target.title: feat: add tool search directory mode
target.body:
## Summary
- Add `tools.toolSearch.mode: "directory"` so large catalogs can expose a compact prompt directory plus `tool_describe`/`tool_call` controls while deferring most schemas.
- Hydrate likely or requested directory tools from the catalog, including missing tool calls, while preserving sequential execution contracts.
- Keep client tools directly visible in directory mode and document the new mode.

Fork attribution: based on JuanHuaXu/openclaw `codex/tool-search-directory-mode`, compared from `codex/fix-post-tool-continuation-guard`.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json packages/agent-core/src/agent-loop.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs packages/agent-core/src/agent-loop.test.ts src/agents/tool-search.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.tool-search-run-plan.test.ts src/config/schema.test.ts -- --reporter=dot`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof
- Behavior or issue addressed: `tools.toolSearch.mode: "directory"` keeps hidden catalog tool schemas deferred while still letting the agent loop hydrate and execute a requested catalog tool through `resolveMissingTool`.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS with Node 24.16.0 after `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`, running the PR branch at `35198cf38b`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran `node --import tsx --input-type=module -` with a throwaway stdin proof script importing `applyToolSchemaDirectoryCatalog`, `buildToolSchemaDirectoryPrompt`, `resolveToolSearchCatalogTool`, and `runAgentLoop`. The script created a directory-mode catalog with `tool_describe`, `tool_call`, one hydrated likely tool, and one deferred hidden tool, then made the model emit a hidden-tool call so `resolveMissingTool` had to hydrate it before execution.
- Evidence after fix:

```json
{
  "mode": "directory",
  "compacted": true,
  "catalogToolCount": 2,
  "visibleToolsBeforeModel": [
    "tool_describe",
    "tool_call",
    "proof_visible_memory"
  ],
  "visibleToolsAfterHydration": [
    "tool_describe",
    "tool_call",
    "proof_visible_memory",
    "proof_hidden_weather"
  ],
  "directoryPromptHasHiddenTool": true,
  "hiddenToolDirectlyVisibleInitially": false,
  "hiddenToolCalls": 1,
  "finalAssistantText": "Directory proof complete."
}
```

- Observed result after fix: Directory mode compacted the catalog; the hidden weather tool appeared in the directory prompt but was not initially exposed as a provider-visible schema, then `resolveMissingTool` hydrated `proof_hidden_weather` and the real agent loop executed it once before producing the final assistant response.
- What was not tested: No live provider API call, packaged install, MCP server, or channel delivery path was exercised in this proof; the focused checks above cover the changed unit/runtime/config surfaces.
